answer:
Front provides an official MCP server at the endpoint https://mcp.frontapp.com/mcp that allows AI agents to interact with Front conversation data [1]. The server utilizes Streamable HTTP transport and requires OAuth 2.1 with PKCE for user-scoped authentication, where an agent's permissions are limited to the specific teammate authorizing the connection [1].

To connect an AI assistant, you must create a Front developer app with the MCP Server feature enabled and configure the necessary scopes, such as read, write, or send [1]. You can then configure your MCP client by providing the server URL and your app's credentials [1]. 

Additionally, FrontMCP is a separate, TypeScript-first framework designed for building MCP servers using decorators and dependency injection, supporting both local and remote deployments with built-in features like session management and hot reloading [2][3][4].
